era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin. Egyptian Old Kingdom (Dynasty VI) under Pepi II. Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. Traditional dating places the Xia Dynasty in China during this period, though archaeological confirmation is debated.
geographic_coverage_gaps: China (Xia Dynasty dating is highly contested)

events.title: Akkadian Empire — administrative and military consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues with consolidation of Akkadian control over Mesopotamia and adjacent regions. Administrative records (clay tablets) document the functioning of the empire's bureaucracy, including tax collection, military organization, and trade. The empire's reach extends from the Persian Gulf to the Mediterranean and Anatolia, though control is increasingly contested.
events.certainty_note: The general state of the Akkadian Empire during Naram-Sin's reign is well-documented; specific events in -2757 are not attested.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is poorly documented even by the standards of the Early Bronze Age. No specific events are recorded for -2757 in surviving sources. The historical record for this period is fragmentary and dominated by royal inscriptions and administrative records from Mesopotamia and Egypt.
